title: Yacht Rental in Marina Del Rey: Costs, Seasons & Top Boats for 2026
html:
Planning a yacht rental in Marina Del Rey for 2026 starts with knowing the options and costs. From Burton Chace Park you can hire a motor yacht for a day on the Pacific, with prices typically from €500 to €5,000. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators and private owners for the widest choice.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marina Del Rey?

Prices range from €150 per person for a shared tour up to €5,000 for a day on a luxury crewed yacht. The cost depends on boat size, season and whether you bring your own skipper. For a clear breakdown, browse the company profiles and read the fine print before booking.

  • Shared sunset tour, €150 per person, 2 hours, max 12 guests
  • Small motorboat (half-day), €400 for 4 hours, no licence required
  • Catamaran (full-day), €1,200, sleeps 10, includes crew
  • Luxury crewed yacht, €3,500, €5,000, 8 hours, champagne and canapés

If you want a fully managed experience, consider skippered charters in Marina Del Rey where the captain handles everything.

Where to set off in Marina Del Rey

The main departure points are Burton Chace Park, Fiji Way and the yacht club docks along the main channel. From here you can head straight out to the open Pacific or cruise the protected marina basins. Nearby spots include:

  • Burton Chace Park, central public dock with visitor parking
  • Marina Beach, sandy cove just east of the marina entrance
  • Playa Vista, residential area with a small boat ramp
  • Mother's Beach, calm lagoon ideal for anchoring
  • Marina del Rey Harbor, main basin with fuel docks and pump-out stations
  • Venice Beach, ten-minute cruise north to the famous boardwalk

Which boat to rent for your Marina Del Rey trip

The best choice depends on group size, budget and whether you want speed or lounging space. Below are the most popular types for this destination.

  1. Bowrider (e.g. Axopar 28), ideal for day trips to Malibu, 8 guests, from €500 half-day
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Princess F70), luxury cruising with crew, 12 guests, from €2,500 half-day. For detailed specs, visit the Prestige 500 charter page which shows a similar 50-foot motor yacht.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), stable and spacious, 10 guests, from €1,000 full-day
  4. Sailboat (e.g. Beneteau Oceanis), quiet and eco-friendly, 6 guests, from €350 half-day
  5. RIB tender, fast shuttle to beaches, 4 guests, from €200 per hour

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marina Del Rey?

In California a boating licence is not required for short-term rentals if the operator provides a skipper. For bareboat hire you need a valid ICC or equivalent, and the rental company will confirm the exact requirements at booking. A local tip: most charter firms offer a free orientation run before you leave the dock.

On busy summer weekends, book your slot in late June or early September when the marine layer burns off by 10am, giving you six hours of calm seas off Santa Monica Bay.

When is the best season for a boat rental in Marina Del Rey?

The peak season runs from May to September, when daytime temperatures reach 25°C and the afternoon sea breeze (the Catalina Eddy) keeps conditions fresh. July and August are the busiest months; booking at least three weeks ahead is wise. Spring and autumn offer quieter anchorages and lower rates, though the water is cooler.

At a glance

  • Price range: €150 to €5,000 per trip
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Typical trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: Up to 12 on most boats
  • Currency: EUR (prices quoted in euros)

Questions about boat rental in Marina Del Rey

Can I rent a boat for a full week in Marina Del Rey?

Yes, several operators offer weekly charters on larger motor yachts and catamarans, with rates from €3,000 for a 40-foot sailboat to €15,000 for a 70-foot luxury yacht.

What happens if seas are rough on the day of my booking?

Your captain will decide based on safety; most companies offer a free reschedule or full refund if conditions are deemed unsafe by the Coast Guard.

Is it possible to take a boat from Marina Del Rey to Catalina Island?

Yes, the 22-nautical-mile crossing to Avalon Harbour takes about 2 hours on a fast motorboat; plan for a full-day trip and check fuel range with the operator.

Do I need to bring my own food and drink on a crewed charter?

No, most crewed charters include a catering service; you can customise the menu when you book, or bring your own if you prefer.

Marina Del Rey offers one of the most accessible bases for yacht rental in Southern California. Whether you choose a self-drive bowrider or a fully crewed motor yacht, the calm waters of the marina and the open Pacific beyond deliver a memorable day afloat. If you own a boat in the area, you can list it on the BoatHire24 marketplace and reach thousands of potential renters. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marina Del Rey or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marina Del Rey and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
